revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Banchō Sarayashiki); add local discussion section + dated import note

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] previous contributors were [[User:en>Artem.G]], [[User:Immanuelle]], [[User:EmmaBot]], see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Banch%C5%8D_Sarayashiki here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:53, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1239557322 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:53, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=C|1= {{WikiProject Japan|culture=y|myth=yes|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Mythology|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Folklore|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject History|importance=Low}} {{WikiProject Theatre|importance = low|attention =}} {{WikiProject Novels|importance=Mid}} {{WikiProject Horror|auto=Inherit|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Death|importance=low}} {{WikiProject Women's History|importance=low}} }} ==Delftware== I am curious if the mention of [[delft]] in the folk version is accurate. Delftware is a dutch type of pottery that purposely imitated oriental patterns (chinese particularly). And they only started imitating chinese patterns in the 1600's. In which case, being imitations, the chinese-pattern delftware would be considered inferior copies by japanese artisans and not a 'family heirloom' as in the story. Not to mention that the story (from all accounts being set in the 17th century ), delftware wouldn't exactly be heirloom, as they would be (then) extremely new fads (and again, the fad for delftware is european, not Japanese). That's kinda like saying ''It is 2004 in [insert country with limited or nonexistent internet connection/computerization] and there was a man named Bob who has an iPod that he treasures greatly because it had been in his family for generations''. '''[[User:Obsidian Soul|Obsidi♠n]][[User talk:Obsidian Soul|Soul]]''' 14:39, 8 November 2010 (UTC) Its possible that it is incorrect. However, at the time Japan was a closed country, so anything of foriegn origin was of great value simply due to the heavy restrictions on imports. [[User:MightyAtom|MightyAtom]] ([[User talk:MightyAtom|talk]]) 23:10, 8 November 2010 (UTC) :I am more likely to assume that it is an incorrect translation though. Probably from someone who didn't know the history and connections between delftware and chinese/japanese ceramics. But yeah, it's not that important to the article overall, heh. '''[[User:Obsidian Soul|Obsidi♠n]][[User talk:Obsidian Soul|Soul]]''' 09:51, 16 January 2011 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:53, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Merge?== I think the merger of [[Okiku]] into this article is a good idea. I think [[User:MightyAtom|MightyAtom]] proposed the merger. A redirect should be left at [[Okiku]], of course. -- [[User:BrianSmithson|BrianSmithson]] 14:33, 18 July 2006 (UTC) : Merge has been done! [[User:MightyAtom|MightyAtom]] 09:27, 3 August 2006 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:53, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == Influnces on Japanese Culture == There is also a Rakugo story called "Okiku's Plates" that is not mentioned in the Influences on Japanese Culture section. http://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%81%8A%E8%8F%8A%E3%81%AE%E7%9A%BF ([[Special:Contributions/144.91.49.79|144.91.49.79]] ([[User talk:144.91.49.79|talk]]) 18:49, 17 July 2011 (UTC)) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 01:53, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
